compiled_state.title: Brainstorm creates follow-on epics without user approval
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

During brainstorm for dso-qo0x, the orchestrator created two follow-on epics without presenting them to the user for approval:

- 8d21-3aa8: Wire recipe library into implementation planning and sprint (Epic B)
- c821-e995: Security remediation recipe catalog

These were created during Phase 3 (Ticket Integration) as part of the epic split and the user's earlier request for a security follow-up. However, neither epic spec was presented to the user via the approval gate before ticket creation.

## How it happened

1. The scope reviewer recommended splitting the epic into A and B. The user approved the split direction ("yes").
2. The user said "security remediation is out of scope, but we should create a follow up epic."
3. The orchestrator interpreted both of these as authorization to create the tickets during Phase 3 ticket integration.

## Why this is wrong

The user approved:
- The SPLIT DIRECTION (yes, split into A and B)
- The CONCEPT of a security follow-up epic

They did NOT approve:
- Epic B's specific success criteria, approach, or scope
- The security epic's specific framing, criteria, or dependencies

The brainstorm approval gate (Phase 2 Step 4) only covers the PRIMARY epic. Follow-on epics created during the same session should each go through their own approval cycle — at minimum a lightweight review of title, description, and dependencies before ticket creation.

Approving the concept of a split is not the same as approving the specific content of both halves. Similarly, "create a follow-up epic for security" authorizes the creation but not the specific framing without review.

## Expected Behavior

When brainstorm creates follow-on or derivative epics:
1. Present each follow-on epic's title, description, and dependencies to the user
2. Get explicit approval before calling ticket create
3. For epics requested by the user (like the security follow-up), present the drafted description for confirmation rather than creating it silently

## Affected Tickets

- 8d21-3aa8: Created without approval of its 5 success criteria
- c821-e995: Created without approval of its description or dependency framing

comments.body: Note: This behavior (creating follow-on epics without user approval) likely occurred after a context compaction event. The brainstorm session was long — by the time Epic B and the security epic were created during Phase 3 ticket integration, the earlier user approval interactions may have been compacted, reducing the orchestrator's sensitivity to the approval-before-creation principle. The orchestrator treated directional approval ('yes, split into A and B') as authorization for specific ticket content without re-presenting for review. Related: c2ac-a305 (misclassification bug).
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Ticket references orchestrator behavior (creating epics without approval gate) AND affected file is skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md. Dual-signal detected. Routing to bot-psychologist.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation complete (bot-psychologist). Root cause: Phase 3 has no approval gate for follow-on/derivative epics — Phase 2 Step 4 approval only covers primary epic. Taxonomy: Multi-Step Reasoning Drift (#7). Hypothesis tests: 1 confirmed. Files affected: brainstorm/SKILL.md (insert after line 412). Proposed fix: insert 'Follow-on and Derivative Epic Gate' HARD-GATE block at start of Phase 3. Confidence: HIGH. Step 3.5: PASS.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing safeguard file(s). Proceeding to fix implementation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Bot-psychologist: Follow-on Epic Gate invisible in step sequence. Fix: renamed to Step 0, updated approval gate pointer.
comments.author: Main
